Meeting
May 22, 2024 - Regular Meeting, 6:00 p.m.
Type
Action (Consent)

WHEREAS, according to NJAC 18A:7B-12.1, the district of residence is responsible for the education of students in a Group Home. When the student attends school in a district other than district of residence, the last district of residence shall pay the cost of tuition for the child to attend school in the other district and pay for any transportation costs incurred by the district. It has been determined that the last district of residence for (S.B.) is Jersey City; and

WHEREAS, on behalf of the Jersey City Public Schools, Warren County has educated this student for the time frames indicated until the family find a permanent residence. Time frame: November 16, 2023 through June 30, 2024.  Total Cost: $12,783.60; now
 
BE IT RESOLVED, the Jersey City Board of Education, upon the recommendation of the Superintendent and Acting School Business Administrator, approves the tuition payment to Hackettstown Board of Education located in Warren County for general education for student (S.B.) who resides in a group home. Total not to exceed $12,783.60 (Pro-rated).  Acct. #11.000.100.561.344.00.000

8.12 Approve National Education Equity Lab School for 2024/25 - Infinity Institute

Meeting
Aug 26, 2024 - Regular Meeting, 6:00 p.m.
Type
Action (Consent)

WHEREAS, Infinity Institute wishes to enroll forty-five (45) high school students in the National Education Equity Lab Program in the Fall and Spring semesters of the 2024/25 school year.  The students will be enrolled in  three online college credit-bearing courses in partnership with Brown University, Howard University, and Stanford University.  The National Education Equity Lab's university partners commit to providing the college credits and university transcript at $250 per student, with a 15 student minimum per course; and WHEREAS, In the fall of 2024, fifteen (15) students will enroll in the course, "Introduction to Environmental Studies", offered by Howard University.  In the Spring of 2025, thirty (30) students will complete the courses, "Between Malcolm X and Martin Luther King, Jr.: Race, Religion, and the Politics of Freedom" and "Introduction to Public Policy: How to Change the World" offered by Stanford University and Brown University, respectively. The total tuition cost for the 2024/25 school year is $11,250.00; andWHEREAS, all students' registration fees will be paid with Title IV funds; now BE IT RESOLVED, the Jersey City Board of Education, upon the recommendation of the Superintendent and Acting School Business Administrator, approve the payment for tuition for the enrollment of forty-five (45) high school students in the National Education Equity Lab Program during the 2024/25 school year at a cost not to exceed $11,250.00.  Pending the release of Title IV Funds; Acct. #20-280-100-500-057-00-0000. Originator:  Jaime Morales, Director

8.72 Approve Tuition Payment to Hoboken Board of Education (G.S.)

Meeting
Jun 26, 2025 - Workshop Meeting, 5:30 pm / Regular Meeting, 6:30 pm
Type
Action (Consent)

WHEREAS, according to N.J.A.C. 18A:7B-12.1, the district of residence is responsible for the education of students in a Group Home. When the student attends school in a district other than the district of residence, the last district of residence shall pay the cost of tuition for the child to attend school in the other district, and pay for any transportation costs incurred by the district.  It has been determined that the last district of residence for (G.S.) is Jersey City; and WHEREAS, on behalf of the Jersey City Public Schools, Hoboken Board of Education has educated this student for the time frame indicated until the family finds a permanent residence. Time frame: September 5, 2024 thru June 30, 2025; nowBE IT RESOLVED, the Jersey City Board of Education, upon the recommendations of the Superintendent and Acting School Business Administrator, approves tuition payment to the Hoboken Board of Education located in the Hudson County, NJ, for general education student (G.S.) in the amount not to exceed $19,800.00; Acct. #11-000-100-561-344-00-000.Originator:  Dr. Joseph Galano, Director

9.07 Approve Tuition Payment to Hoboken Board of Education (A.R.)

Meeting
Mar 26, 2026 - Regular Meeting, 5:30 pm
Type
Action (Consent)

WHEREAS, according to NJAC 18A:7B-12.1 the district of residence is responsible for the education of students in a Group Home. When the student attends school in a district other than district of residence, the last district of residence shall pay the cost of tuition for the child to attend school in the other district and pay for any transportation costs incurred by the district. It has been determined that the last district of residence for (A.R.) is Jersey City; andWHEREAS, on behalf of the Jersey City Public Schools, Hoboken Board of Education has educated this student for the time frames indicated until the family finds a permanent residence.  Time frame: September 4, 2025 thru June 30, 2026; now, therefore BE IT RESOLVED, the Jersey City Board of Education, upon the recommendations of the Superintendent and the School Business Administrator, approves the tuition payment to Hoboken Board of Education located in Hudson County for general education student (A.R.) in the amount not to exceed $21,629.00; Acct. 11-000-100-561-344-00-000.Originator:  Dr. Joseph Galano, Director
